hiring-and-team-management/application-p.title: Application Process
hiring-and-team-management/application-p.content:
# Application Process

# Application Process

---

We recommend a 4-step hiring process: Job Posting → Application Form → Loom Video → Interview

If you like someone in a step, invite them to the next step by telling them you want them to move to the next hiring stage, and send them the link. This funnel is just a filtering process to save you tons of time. You want to end up interviewing 3-8 people, after getting 50-200 job applications. Each step weeds out those who are lazy, incompetent, or unqualified.

# **Job Posting**

- Give them all the info they would want to know, and also give them all the info you want them to know about the job
- Give them specific instructions to apply, so you can see if they follow them or not
- You will get a lot of applications, so do not spend too much time reading each one. You will just be scanning their answers to see if they followed your instructions and have decent experience (you will learn more about their skills and experience in the application form)

# **Application Form**

- Use Google Forms or Typeform to create a free application form
- Watch out for these red flags:
    - Rushed/1-sentence answers
    - Lack of experience or skills
    - Poor English
    - Incompetence

- These are the questions used for an Inbox Manager role:
    - Email
    - First and Last Name
    - City or Town
    - Post a link to your Resume/CV
    - Do you have access to a web camera and microphone for the duration of your shift?
    - What is your internet speed? Post a screenshot of proof.
    - If you lose internet at your house, do you have other means of using the internet? How?
    - Are you currently working? If so, explain your current job and it's hours.

	**WORK EXPERIENCE & SKILLS**

- Describe [**briefly**] your experience working as a VA.*

Give details about how many people/companies you've worked with, how long, and what your tasks were.

- Describe [**in detail**] your experience with email/chat support.
- Describe [**in detail**] your experience as an SDR/in sales/in business development/with lead generation
- Do you have experience communicating with leads or prospects in a B2B setting, rather than communicating with customer inquiries? If so, explain [**in detail**].

**OTHER WORK EXPERIENCE & SKILLS**

- Do you have experience in sending cold emails, and/or setting up email campaigns? If so, explain [**in detail**].
- Do you have experience in 'scrapping' (building lead lists)? This could be done manually, like searching Instagram for leads, or it could be done with software, like using LinkedIn Sales Nav. If so, explain [**in detail**].
- Do you have experience in 'scrapping' (building lead lists)? This could be done manually, like searching Instagram for leads, or it could be done with software, like using LinkedIn Sales Nav. If so, explain [**in detail**].
- Do you have experience in organizing spreadsheets, or using Google sheets? If so, explain [**in detail**].
- Do you have experience in email tech/backend work. For example, setting up DNS records, forwarding domains, etc. If so, explain [**in detail**].*
- **For the following software, explain your knowledge and/or experience with them. If you don't have any, just say 'None'.**

If you have experience with similar software, explain.

- Apollo.io

A lead-list database software like CrunchBase, Zoominfo, etc.

- Instantly.ai

A platform that sends cold emails. Similar to Mailshake, Lemlist, Snovio, etc.

- Zapier

An automation software that automatically completes a variety of administrative tasks.

- Notion*

It's hard to describe what Notion is if you aren't aware of it. It's like Google drive but can do a lot more things.

**HOURS & WAGES**

- How many hours are you looking for a week?*

This position is currently hiring for between 20-40 hours a week. Preferably 40 (Fulltime)

- What times of the day would you be available? Use Mountain Time.
- What days of the week would you be available? Use Mountain Time.*

This position is Monday-Friday. However if you are available for weekends there may be work from time to time.

- What hourly rate do you request to accept this job [in $ USD]*

Feel free to explain your reasoning if you wish. The starting wage is $4.00 /hour, however that rate can be increased with adequate work experience.

**FINAL QUESTIONS**

- Why do you think you'd be a good fit for this position?
- What is your one biggest strength that would help you be successful in the position?
- Give one weakness of yours. Something you want to improve upon.
- Are you willing to do other type of work if needed?*

Other types of work could include working with email lists, prospect outreach, image editing, researching, etc. You do not need any experience, any additional work you would be completely trained for.

- This job requires you to communicate with prospects through email. Are your written and oral English skills good enough to communicate with prospects?*

No voice communication with prospects is required.

- Is there anything I should know about you specifically with regards to this job? Any extra information, details or specifics, limitations, etc.
- Who would win in a fight between a tiger and a gorilla? Explain why.*

There is no wrong answer

# **Loom Video**

- Make them record a 3 minute (no longer) video going over 3-5 of your main questions
- Purpose: You get to see and hear them without scheduling a 30 minute interview. You will be able to immediately disqualify people after listening to them speak for just 2 minutes. They also must explain their answer more thoroughly when they speak, and a lot more truth about their skills and experience will come out
- Tell them Loom has a free version and a paid version, and they can use the free version to do this
- Do not tell them how. If they cannot figure out how to use Loom, they are not a good fit.

# **Interview**

- Make a Calendly event just for your interviews. Make it so it’s only open for a 2 day window. Make sure there are plenty of times throughout the day so they can find a time that works.
- Most of them are going to be nervous. Try and put them at ease. This is not just to be a good person, but you want them to open up about their skills and experience. Many people in the Philippines need prodding to open up. It’s strange, but sometimes they are too nervous to be confident in their skills.
- You will already have a good idea of each candidate before you talk, you’ve read their application and watched their video. The interview is where you can ask specific questions and go back and forth, so you can get more information.

- These are questions used to hire a Google Ads Manager. You would change the questions to make them more relevant to your position.
    - Why are you interested in this position?
    - How many hours a week do you want to work?
- **Other Employers**
    - Do you have another job right now?
    - If so, will you be leaving that job or working at both?
    - If so, will it conflict with this job?
    - Why are you not working there full time, and instead looking to work at 2 part time jobs?
    - Describe the relationship you have with your current employer. How do you communicate with them, how do they give you work?
- **Google Ads Experience**
    - How many years experience do you have with Google Ads?
    - How many client accounts have you worked on?
    - When you have been working on client accounts, how much did you do yourself and how much did other people do with you?
    - What kind of clients have you found the most success with?
    - What kind of research do you do before starting a build?
    - What is the structure of your builds? How do you build campaigns? How many keywords go into an ad group?
    - How many campaigns can you handle with (20/40) hours per week? Making sure every account gets complete attention and no account is ever neglected?
- **Working With Me**
    - Explain what I need from them*
    - Are you able to explain and teach your processes to me?
    - Are you ok working in a new company with an inexperienced leader?
- **Personal Life**
    - Tell me about where you live and your family.
    - What do you like to do outside of work?
    - Tell me about a hard decision you had to make recently.
- **Wrap Up**
    - What hourly rate are you hoping to get for this position?
    - Why do you think that hourly rate is fair?
    - Do you have any questions for me?
    - I have interviews until the end of Friday night. I will make my decision that night or the next day, and reach out to the winning candidate with an offer. When an offer with a candidate is agreed upon, I will inform the other candidates that the position has been filled.

# Employee Onboarding & Training

# Employee Onboarding & Training

---

# **Expectations & KPIs**

Set these on their first day during onboarding:

- Show up on time
- Respect the company and its interests
- Follow directions carefully
- Always ask for help, even if they’ve been told 8 times
- Respond to leads within 5 min (or whatever job-specific KPIs are required, this is a KPI for an inbox manager)
- Take accountability of the work

# **SOPs**

SOPs are the core of onboarding new employees.

Employees can refer to these (repeatedly) which is great for them, and vastly reduces the time spent b*y you*

Use our resources for any SOPs you may need.

# **Onboarding**

Give them all the logins needed to do their work.

Give them a quick and easy way to contact you. We recommend Slack or WhatsApp.

Go over their main work with them, even if you have SOPs. It’s important for them to understand how you want things done. They will also be nervous and want to do their work well, and it gives them confidence to be shown what to do 1-on-1.

Make sure they have all of these things, and you will be giving them everything they need to excel:

- The tools needed to do their job (software, logins, etc)
- The resources needed for their job (SOPs, guides, etc)
- Clear tasks, goals, or KPIs for them to work on (if they don’t know what they should always be doing, that is stressful for them, and wastes the time you’re paying them for)
- A method to contact you directly, for direction or assistance
- A positive work environment (a good boss, good coworkers)
- A sense of accomplishment, people want to feel like they are doing good work and contributing
- A reliable source of income [Most important, never, ever be late in paying them. Ever.]

hiring-and-team-management/general-on-hi.content:
# General on hiring

# **General on hiring**

---

Hiring your first employee is a big step. Do it right, and you set your agency up for growth. Here’s what you need to know:

### **1. Be Clear on What You Need**

- **Define the Role:** Write a job description that clearly lists tasks, responsibilities, and required skills. Don't assume candidates will "figure it out." Clarity saves time.
- **Test for Fit:** Before committing, assign a small, paid task to see how they work. This ensures they can do the job and fit your style.

### **2. Treat People Well**

- **Be Honest:** Set clear expectations about the job, salary, and hours. Don't overpromise.
- **Respect Their Time:** Pay on time, provide feedback, and acknowledge good work. If they’re treated well, they’ll deliver better results.
- **Cultural Sensitivity:** If you’re hiring globally, especially from countries like the Philippines or Lebanon, take the time to understand cultural differences. This builds trust and prevents miscommunication.

### **3. Move Quickly**

- **Hire Fast:** When you find someone who fits your criteria, don’t hesitate. Waiting too long risks losing good candidates.
- **Fire Faster (When Necessary):** If someone isn’t meeting expectations despite your efforts to guide and support them, make the call to let them go. Keeping the wrong person drains your time and resources.

### **4. Build a Simple System**

- **Use Tools:** Platforms like Upwork, OnlineJobs.ph, or Fiverr make finding candidates easier. Leverage these to cast a wide net and narrow down the best fits.
- **Document Processes:** Once someone is hired, provide written guides or videos to help them get started. This saves time and ensures consistency.

### **5. Be a Leader**

- **Set the Tone:** Employees look to you for direction. Be clear, consistent, and available to answer questions.
- **Foster Trust:** Share your vision and involve them in your agency’s success. Motivated employees are invested employees.

# Where To Hire From?

We recommend onlinejobs.ph to hire any VA roles, like inbox managers, list builders, admin, etc. You can also you [Upwork.com](http://Upwork.com) or Fiverr. 

You can also use online groups to source talent, like Instantly.ai’s Facebook group. 

## **OnlineJobs.PH**

**Pros:**

- A huge database of people’s profiles
- The best search feature/filters to find the right people
- Lots of people have experience in all the roles an agency needs (specifically inbox managing, list building, admin work, and even running email campaigns)
- They are looking for full-time and permanent work

**Cons:**

- Only available to hire people in the Philippines (this could be considered a Pro to some people)
- You need to pay $69

## **Upwork**

**Pros:**

- Lots of people from many countries are available to hire
- Great filters to find specific types of employees
- Free to use post and message potential employees

**Cons:**

- Upwork wants you to keep everything within their site, especially payment. It’s against policy to use their site to connect and then arrange payment outside. We officially do not recommend you break this policy. We also comment that a large amount of people do break this policy every day with no consequences.

## **Fiverr**

Pros:

- Lots of people from many countries are available to hire

Cons:

- Fiverr focuses more on “gigs” which are one-time or short-term projects, rather than hiring someone full-time. However it is still very possible to find someone willing to work full time

# Compensation Structure

When deciding on a compensation model, keep it simple and motivating. We recommend either offering a **base salary with a commission per booked call** or going with a **fully base salary**. 

The base salary + commission model incentivizes performance and is highly effective for roles like appointment setters, where output is directly tied to results. It also reduces your risk as an employer and agency owner. However, it can create a moral hazard. Employees may focus too heavily on booking as many calls as possible—even unqualified ones—to maximize their commission. This focus on commissions can lead them to neglect responsibilities like reporting, quality control, or other essential tasks that don’t directly impact their earnings. 

On the other hand, a fully base salary provides stability and more security for the employees. It allows employees to prioritise quality without the pressure of commissions.

# **How much is a VA?**

You will be pay between $4-8 for a VA who is not extremely specialised. There are lots of VAs with great experience who charge more. But most VAs you will be an hourly rate between $5-7. 

### **How much is that full-time?**

$6 x 8 (hrs per day) = $48/day

$48 x 21 (avg biz days/mo) = $1,008/mo

# **How do I track their time?**

We highly recommend [Hubstaff](https://hubstaff.com/?utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=TT_AF_Search&utm_content=S_Branded&utm_term=hubstaff&gad_source=1&gclid=Cj0KCQiAhbi8BhDIARIsAJLOluejQy7wrux4LYhywfq7WjJvX44IWvPlU809gWc4YK1oYHjqkuZhJTEaAv3eEALw_wcB). It tracks their time and lets you know exactly how much they’re owed.

- Track time
- Set shift schedules + breaks
- Screenshots every 10 min (you can check these occasionally to ensure they aren’t working for someone else during their shift with you)
- Mouse activity (turn this off)
- Timesheets showing exact amount to pay

You can also use [Clockify](https://clockify.me/) for a cheaper and simpler alternative. 

# **How do I send them their money?**

We recommend paying employees through Wise.

- Lowest fees for employees (and you)
- Quickest transfer speeds for employees
- Numerous ways for you to deposit funds
- Reliable, always works
    - [Don’t ever mess around with payroll]

# Hiring SDRs

# Hire Cold Callers from South Africa

### **1. Create a LinkedIn Job Posting:**

- Use **LinkedIn Jobs** to advertise the position, targeting South Africa.
- **Boost the Job Post**:
    - Allocate **£20/day** to boost the listing for better visibility.
    - Set the job title as something clear and appealing, e.g., *Cold Caller with Sales Experience* or *Outbound Sales Representative*.

### **2. Define Job Requirements:**

- Offer a competitive salary:
    - **Base Pay:** Advertise a monthly salary of **£1000** for experienced candidates.
    - **On-Target Earnings (OTE):** Mention a potential OTE of **£1500+** to attract motivated candidates.
    - **Optional Lower Budget:** You can offer a lower base (e.g., £800/month) but expect a trade-off in quality and more management overhead.
- Emphasize the need for **direct cold calling experience** and strong communication skills.

### **3. Application Screening:**

- After **2–3 days**, you can expect **200+ applicants** if the post is optimized.
- Filter for applicants with:
    - Direct cold-calling or outbound sales experience.
    - Clear, professional LinkedIn profiles with evidence of past results.

### **4. Shortlist Applicants:**

- Select the top candidates based on their profiles and relevant experience.
- Reject those without clear cold-calling experience to save time.

### **5. Initial Screening with a Loom Video:**

- Send shortlisted candidates an email asking them to:
    - Record a **1–2 minute Loom video** introducing themselves.
    - Answer specific questions like:
        - Why are you interested in this role?
        - Share an example of a successful cold-calling experience.
        - How do you handle objections on calls?

### **6. Interview the Best Candidates:**

- Schedule Zoom or phone interviews with the top Loom video submissions.
- During the interview:
    - Have them read a **prepared sales pitch** aloud to assess their tone, clarity, and confidence.
    - Role-play a cold call scenario to evaluate their skills and adaptability.

### **7. Final Selection:**

- Evaluate candidates based on:
    - Cold calling pitch delivery.
    - Overall professionalism and enthusiasm.
    - Experience and understanding of your sales objectives.
- Offer the position to the best fit, clearly outlining expectations and compensation structure.

### **8. Onboarding:**

- Provide thorough training on:
    - Your product/service.
    - Your cold-calling script and objection-handling techniques.
- Set up tools for tracking performance (e.g., CRM, call tracking software).
- Monitor initial calls and provide feedback for improvement.

By following this process, you can efficiently hire experienced cold callers from South Africa while ensuring quality and cost-effectiveness.

# Job Posting Template

# Job Posting Template

---

Explain what they would be doing, in a way they would understand it (their responsibilities, and their most common tasks). Make this as clear as you can. For example, if you’re hiring an inbox manager they might not know what that is. But if you say it’s B2B email chat support, appointment setting, or SDR email lead reply handling, they may have years of experience.

Explain who your company is and who you are. Give them an idea of what it will be like to work with you. Almost all of them are used to working with big agencies or in callcenters, where they have dozens of coworkers. Most will be surprised and interested in working for a company of 2-5.

State the time zone you are in, and make sure they focus on your time, not theirs.  This makes it much easier for you, and if they fail to listen to your instructions they are discarded.

You need to give them specific and detailed instructions to apply. The reason is you are simply seeing if they follow your instructions or not. If they do not follow instructions during the application, why would you hire them? If you tell them to change the subject line of their application to “silly dinosaur”, but the subject line says something else, you know they are not a good person to hire.

**Make a copy of the template here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1gM3eHCLfk31uobk7MnW4QeyKac3CA_RB5-9qHBWZc5Y/edit?usp=sharing**

# Campaign FAQs # Campaign FAQs --- ### Should I track open rates?
⚠️ **We recommend not tracking opens. Track performance using the reply rate instead**
**Use this checklist to identify what the issue is:** 1. Subject Line 2. Sending domain 3. Sending Volume 4. Sending Times 5. Email List Cleanliness 6. Targeting 7. DKIM, SPF, and DMARC Records 8. Workspace Settings 9. Spam Trigger Words in Subject Line 10. A/B Testing of Subject Lines and Sending Times ### First lines - worth it?
Generally - No. But scrape 1000 leads for your first campaign. Split it into 2 batches of 500 leads. One with first-lines and one without. Compare the results and see if it’s worth adding first-lines to justify the cost.
### **What are Some Effective Subject Lines for Increasing Open Rates?** **For effective subject lines, you should:** - Keep them short and intriguing. - Use personalised and relevant information. - Create a sense of urgency or exclusivity. - A/B test different subject lines.
💡 **Try out these subject lines** - quick question {first name} - quick question {company name} - question about {company name} - {your company name} + {their company name} - {firstname}, 10 calls with your ideal clients on us - Does {company name} you need more leads? - struggling with churn rate?
### **What are Some A/B Testing Strategies for Improving Email Performance?**
For A/B testing: - Test one variable at a time like subject lines, send times, or CTA buttons. - Use a significant sample size. - Analyze results based on key metrics like open rates, click-through rates, and conversion rates.
### **How Do I Handle Unsubscribes and Opt-Outs in Email Campaigns?** - Include a clear and easy-to-find unsubscribe link in every email. - Promptly honour unsubscribe requests. - Use opt-out information to refine your email strategy and targeting. ### **What are the Key Metrics to Measure the Success of Email Outreach?**
Key metrics for email outreach are: - Reply Rate - Bounce Rate - Positive Reply Rate - Meetings Booked Per Emails Sent
### **How Can I Reduce the Chances of My Emails Being Marked as Spam?**
Follow our resources and you’ll be fine [Writing Emails](/resources/writing-emails-9bc75337ac584d679d465712848be02c)
### What should my email signature look like? **Your name** **Position | Company** **Phone (not needed)** **Address (Address improves deliverability)** ### Should I send to personal email addresses? Ideally, no. However, in certain industries like local businesses, it can be challenging to avoid sending to personal email addresses. Keep in mind, though, that this approach may accelerate inbox burnout. ### Should I contact multiple decision-makers from the same company? Yes, If the business has more than 25 employees ### Should I manually schedule calls or use a calendar link? You will see a much higher call conversion rate if you manually reply to prospects to arrange calls as opposed to sending a calendar link Example: [https://twitter.com/sean0to10k/status/1516098805774565382?s=20&t=lySMelgi9cRlBj_SQhwciQ](https://twitter.com/sean0to10k/status/1516098805774565382?s=20&t=lySMelgi9cRlBj_SQhwciQ) ### Find the best time to send emails to decision-makers [Email Time Optimizer - Growbots Labs](https://growbots.com/labs/time-optimizer/) ### How to manage 10+ Gmail inboxes on Chrome [https://www.loom.com/share/adb121649fca4974b57282373425cbcd?sharedAppSource=personal_library](https://www.loom.com/share/adb121649fca4974b57282373425cbcd?sharedAppSource=personal_library) On this page

# DNS FAQs # DNS FAQ --- ### **How to change the profile picture in Google Workspace** [https://www.loom.com/share/65cb6f20757c458995c871e19497d12f](https://www.loom.com/share/65cb6f20757c458995c871e19497d12f) ### **How to forward your outreach domains to your main domain** [https://app.tango.us/app/workflow/How-to-forward-your-outreach-domains-to-your-main-domain-bab4018c861c4a8a904617d2dbaf8683](https://app.tango.us/app/workflow/How-to-forward-your-outreach-domains-to-your-main-domain-bab4018c861c4a8a904617d2dbaf8683) ### **How to set up DMARC** --- Put this record in your DNS section of your domain: **Type**: TXT **Host**: _dmarc **Value**: “v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:name@domain.com”
👉 **Instead of name@domain.com put your email.**
### **How to filter out DMARC reports from your inbox** [https://www.loom.com/share/fd0774862f2e440faa383710e0262074](https://www.loom.com/share/fd0774862f2e440faa383710e0262074)
👉 **DMARC senders for you to filter out:**
👉 noreply-dmarc-support@google.com
👉 dmarc_reports@reports.emailsrvr.com
👉 noreply-dmarc@zoho.com
👉 dmarcreport@microsoft.com
👉 abuse@seznam.cz
### **Tools for Checking DNS Records** **DKIM Record Checker** [https://mxtoolbox.com/dkim.aspx](https://mxtoolbox.com/dkim.aspx) **DMARC Record Checker** [https://mxtoolbox.com/dmarc.aspx?referrer=dmarctools-content](https://mxtoolbox.com/dmarc.aspx?referrer=dmarctools-content) [https://mxtoolbox.com/spf.aspx](https://mxtoolbox.com/spf.aspx) ### **Best Practices For 2FA [Avoiding Common Mistakes, Giving Your Team Access, Where To Get Numbers]** [https://www.loom.com/share/fbf4cc57308e41b6937377a4a7765ec3](https://www.loom.com/share/fbf4cc57308e41b6937377a4a7765ec3) ### **What are Some Best Practices for Setting Up DKIM, SPF, and DMARC Records?** - For setting up DKIM, SPF, and DMARC records: - Create a DKIM record in your domain’s DNS settings and configure it with your ESP. - Update your domain’s DNS with an SPF record indicating which mail servers are authorised to send email on your behalf. - Configure a DMARC record in your DNS settings to specify how receivers should handle emails that fail SPF or DKIM checks. ← Previous [FAQs](../Frequently%20Asked%20Questions%20(FAQs)%2013af6d94771f8112973cd149c3f10647.md) Next → [Lead Scraping FAQs](/resources/lead-scraping-faqs-e364f7e7384a47b780964bf260ec7cce) On this page

key-resources/frequently-asked-questions.content:
# Inbox & Deliverability FAQs # Inbox & Deliverability FAQs --- ### Where should I get mobile numbers from to verify Google accounts? - [**https://www.textverified.com](https://www.textverified.com/) (Recommended)** - [**http://sms-activate.org**](http://sms-activate.org/) - [**http://smspva.com**](http://smspva.com/) --- ### Should I continue to warm inboxes even after 4 days? No --- ### **How Can I Improve My Email Deliverability Rate?**
ℹ️ To improve your email deliverability rate: - Authenticate your emails with DKIM, SPF, and DMARC. - Regularly clean your email list, removing bounced and unengaged emails. - Use a reputable Email Service Provider (ESP). - Avoid spam trigger words and follow the CAN-SPAM Act requirements. To identify and avoid spam trigger words, consider using a tool like MailTester.
--- ### What to do when your Google Workspace Account disconnects from Instantly (App Password) - Think if you’ve recently changed your password (That may be the cause) - Whether or not you have though, Go inside Google and create a new app password. - Wait a few hours and then re-add the account to Instantly with the new app password. --- --- ### What to do if Google doesn’t let you use a phone number from Hushed to set up 2FA - Use Skype number --- ### Where to get backup codes to Gmail accounts if you have 2FA set up? - Generally it’s a good idea to get your codes every time for every account with 2FA - If you lose the phone number you won’t be able to get the account back not even with Google’s support. - You can find the backup codes option in Gmail account settings (Manage your account → Security), under 2-Step Verification --- ## What to do when Gmail locks your account and you get a warning --- ![Untitled](Inbox%20&%20Deliverability%20FAQs/Untitled.png) ![Screenshot 2024-01-31 at 10.05.18.png](Inbox%20&%20Deliverability%20FAQs/Screenshot_2024-01-31_at_10.05.18.png) - In most cases, you would not actually need to appeal - Log in as you normally would to your admin panel (admin.google.com) - When asked for number verification, you can get a US-based number from [textverified.com](http://textverified.com) or sms-man.com. Skype and Hushed numbers do not work for these one-time verification requests. It needs to be a sim-based number. - Under Users, restore the restricted account - Lower your sending volume - See if you got any very negative replies. If you did, make sure to edit your email script accordingly ← Previous [Lead Scraping FAQs](/resources/lead-scraping-faqs-e364f7e7384a47b780964bf260ec7cce) Next → [Campaign FAQs](/resources/campaign-faqs-d574dd8203b2493fb80aee18db8d814e) On this page

# How To Setup Your LinkedIn Profile # How To Setup Your LinkedIn Profile James’ masterclass is the best resource we’ve seen so far. So make sure to go through it and take notes [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2koA3ghetOE&feature=youtu.be](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2koA3ghetOE&feature=youtu.be) ## Steps 1. **Profile Picture -** make it look professional 2. **Headline -** needs to tell people what you do and show some of your achievements relevant to the niche 3. **Header Image -** use it as a space to provide additional information about what you do or ideally social proof 4. **About Summary -** describe what you do, for who, what results you have achieved and what people can expect after working with you 5. **Featured Section -** the best way to use this section is to highlight your case studies, if you have some video case studies put them there 6. **Experience Section** 7. **Skills & endorsements - pick the most relevant skills as shown 3, use the others as SEO keywords** 8. **Recommendations - if you worked with someone in the past ask for a recommendation, make it describe the transformation they went through when working with you** 9. **Interests & Groups** 10. **The Account Admin Section**
⚠️ **If you don’t have at least 500 connections…**
- Use [**https://www.linkedjetpack.com/**](https://www.linkedjetpack.com/) and buy 500 connections - Use it to buy the $99 package of endorsements as well
💡 If you can’t use your LinkedIn profile for whatever reason (job etc), make a new one as a fake person working at your company. We recommend you buy an avatar from [getaia.com](https://getaia.io/) as if it gets ID verification they will give you a new one Get 20% OFF Use code “CCG”

linkedin/how-to-use-linkedin-voice-messa.content:
# How to Use LinkedIn Voice Messages # How to Use LinkedIn Voice Messages How many LinkedIn voice messages have you ever received? Probably not that many (if any). It’s a new channel that will automatically grab a person’s attention just because of its novelty - people are curious! ## How To Send Voice Messages? ![](https://i.imgur.com/DaMotum.gif) ## When To Use Them ### **1. Use it as an unconventional first touch** The idea is to leverage this less conventional channel in your first touch and make a great first impression. ### **2. Use it as a soft reminder within a sales sequence** **Here is an example of what you could say in this situation:** > “Hey {{firstname}}, I just shot you an email but figured dropping you a message to explain why I’m reaching out to you. I saw that you are {{title}} at {companyname}} and figured that [topic] falls into your wheelhouse. Are you the right person to discuss this with?” > ### **3. Use it as an unexpected last touch** **Here is an example of what you could say in this situation:** > “Hi {{first_name}}, I thought I’d give it a last shot. We’re currently working with… Do you think this is worth a conversation?” > ## Best Practices ### **1. Do your research and connect with the prospect beforehand (2-3 days)** ### **2. Structure your voice message** - **Be concise.** Although LinkedIn allows you to send 1-minute long messages, keep your message under 30 seconds. - **Have a powerful and personalized intro (first 7-10 secs).** You want to get the prospect’s attention in the first 5 seconds. Prospects don’t like to lose time with spammers, so make sure to leverage your research and personalize your message. > “Hey {{first_name}}, I figured I’d drop you a message as I’m sure that everyone is probably texting you at this moment.”“Hi {{firstname}}, I looked at your profile and saw that you are {{title}} at {{companyname}}. I figured that [topic] falls into your wheelhouse.” > - **Introduce your value proposition (next 15 secs).** Prove that you’re worth continuing to listen to. Follow the introduction with a strong message that provides value. > “We just got off a call with a client we recently helped solve [issue] for in only a matter of weeks.”“We’re currently helping clients with [problemyousolve] and I was curious to see if this is also something you’ve been facing recently.” > - **End with an effective call to action (5-10 secs).** The end goal of your message is to get the prospect to engage with you. Make sure you offer a chance to get a reply. > “I would love to schedule a call to go over this issue, would you be the best person to discuss this with?" > ### **3. Record, listen and send your message!** When you practice, record it so you can listen to yourself. Make sure you sound conversational, not too formal nor too casual. Speak in a clear tone and don’t rush the script (even though it’s only 30 secs you’ll see that you have time to say all the relevant things you need). When you're ready, **hit send!** - **Example script you can use**
ℹ️ Hey {{first_name}}! Hope you’re having a great day. I noticed that you’re in {{niche}}. If I told you {{offer}} {{*leadgen example:* I could get you 10-15 more meetings with your ideal clients every single month}}, is that something you’d be open to hearing more about?
### **4. Follow-up with text** Prospects aren’t usually aware of this functionality, so they might be skeptical to open your voice message. Send them a personalized very short text to make sure they are prompted to open your recording. > “Hi {{first_name}}, just dropped a quick message over here, keen to hear back.” > ← Previous [Boolean Search](https://www.notion.so/2aec98e98ade4ed2b294b5fbacef859b?pvs=21) Next → [Additional Resources](https://www.notion.so/d26409ff408e4d24a49ca7a996fd6cc8?pvs=21)

linkedin/how-to-use-boolean-searches-65d.content:
# How to use Boolean searches

# **How to use Boolean searches**

Ever tried to perform a Linkedin search and thousands of results come up but none of them are what you’re looking for?
**There’s a quick fix for that:** Boolean searches

### What is a Boolean search?

A Boolean search is a search on LinkedIn that uses logical operators. It uses specific logic using 5 key operators. 
Using these elements will let you narrow down your searches. So you’ll get more specific results

### The 5 elements of the syntax are

---

1. **OR**
You can type “OR” between two words of your search and you should receive the results from
both keywords.
Example:
You can do a search for: “CEO” OR “Owner” OR “Partner”. You should get a list of people who
are either a CEO, owner or partner of a business.

---

1. **NOT**
Typing “NOT” (all caps) before the searched keyword will exclude any profiles that include it
from the results. You can also use the minus sign (-) instead of NOT to achieve the same result.
Example:
“Marketing Manager” NOT “sales”. This type of search will give you a much more accurate list of
people related to marketing excluding people that are in sales

---

1. **Parenthesis searches**
Using parentheses in your search query, you tell the search engine how to handle the order of
importance. This is essential when combining AND and OR queries.
Example:
(“CEO” AND “Managing Director) OR “Owner”. When you do this type of search, it will include
only those profiles that mention both “CEO” and “Managing Director” at the same time, or those
that mention just “Owner”.

---

1. **AND**
In order for this operator to work, you have to type it in all caps - “AND”. Typing it in lower case
won’t work.
Example:
“Sales Manager” AND “Insurance”. This will bring up results for people that are sales managers
in the insurance industry.

---

1. **Quotation Marks**
Using quotation “ “ marks around certain phrases will show you only results that exactly match
your query.
Example:
“Business Development Manager”. Typing it this way will only give you search results for people
that have a business development manager position mentioned on their profile.
Using the 5 Boolean Linkedin search operators will help you create appropriate lists of your
leads. So you can get targeted, more specific people and make your emails convert better.
You can then use a scraping software such as Client Scraper to scrape these lists.

# Documentation Use cases # Documentation > Aether is perfect for building out documentation of all kinds, check out the content below for examples on what can be achieved. >
⚡ [Check out the official Super documentation built using Aether here](https://docs.super.so)
## Code Documentation ### Environment Variables Aether has built-in support for loading environment variables into the browser and Functions. Loading environment variables into Node.js requires a small code snippet. In development, Aether will load environment variables from a file named `.env.development`. For builds, it will load from `.env.production`. A `.env` file could look like: ``` Aether=https://dev.example.com/apiAPI_KEY=927349872349798 ``` To load these into Node.js, add the following code snippet to the top of your `Aether-config.js` file: ```jsx require("dotenv").config({ path: `.env.${process.env.NODE_ENV}`,}) ``` This loads `process.env.AETHER_API_URL` and `process.env.API_KEY` for use in `Aether-*.js` files and functions. For example, when configuring a plugin in `aether-config.js`: ```jsx require("dotenv").config({ path: `.env.${process.env.NODE_ENV}`, }) module.exports = { plugins: [ { resolve: `aether`, options: { apiKey: process.env.API_KEY, }, }, ], } ``` ### **Accessing Environment Variables in the browser.** By default, environment variables are only available in Node.js code and are not available in the browser as some variables should be kept secret and not exposed to anyone visiting the site. To expose a variable in the browser, you must preface its name with `AETHER_`. So `AETHER_API_URL` will be available in browser code but `API_KEY` will not. Variables are set when JavaScript is compiled so when the development server is started or you build your site. ```jsx import React, { useState, useEffect } from "react" function App() { const [data, setData] = useState() useEffect(async () => { const result = await fetch( `${process.env.AETHER_API_URL}/users` ).then(res => res.json()) setData(result.data) }) return (
    {data.map(user => (
  • {user.name}
    • ))}
) } export default App ``` ### **Add `.env*` files to .gitignore** Environment variable files should not be committed to Git as they often contain secrets which are not safe to add to Git. Instead, add `.env.*` to your `.gitignore` file and setup the environment variables manually locally. --- ## Reference Tables ### Light mode colors [Light Mode](Documentation/Light%20Mode%20142f6d94771f8180af72ff7c73382f0f.csv) ### Dark mode colors [Dark Mode](Documentation/Dark%20Mode%20142f6d94771f81cb9d81c3507a695256.csv) --- ## Guides and Instructions ### Adding a custom domain to your site To add a domain to your site, first head into your site editor (cog icon) and click into the **"Domains"** page, then click the **"Add a custom domain"** button. After entering your own domain address in the popup, you'll then need to make some changes to the DNS settings through your hosting provider. You would need to do this through your domain registrar's (GoDaddy, Namecheap, Google Domains, etc) or DNS provider's (Cloudflare, Netlify, etc) website. For specific instructions, view the content inside the toggle blocks below. ## DNS Settings Below you can find the DNS settings that need to be included on your domain registrar's (GoDaddy, Namecheap, Google Domains, etc) or DNS provider's (Cloudflare, Netlify, etc) website. ### Root domain records For a root domain like `example.com` you'll need to add the following records: [Untitled](Documentation/Untitled%20142f6d94771f81048261c610ec6a4f67.csv) ### Subdomain records For a subdomain like `blog.example.com` add this record: [Untitled](Documentation/Untitled%20142f6d94771f81c4bde8da5e1fe6f6d3.csv) **If you run into issues, please make sure that you have:** - Added new DNS records to your domain provider - Removed old DNS records - Waited up to 24 hours for new settings to propagate
💡 This tool can be used to check if the records are set up correctly: [https://www.whatsmydns.net/](https://www.whatsmydns.net/)

# Using Aether

Getting started

[Using Aether](/resources/using-aether-142f6d94771f819286ebc3cff7a9f22a)

[Documentation](/resources/documentation-142f6d94771f81e1baf4d12323639e91)

# Using Aether

> Aether comes with some additional custom features
> 

## Left sidebar

The left hand sidebar, by default lists all the pages in your site and is wrapped in a synced block so that when you modify the content inside it will update throughout your site. This sidebar **will not** be visible on mobile devices so it is important to add page links to the mobile menu via the Super Navbar, learn more in the next step ↓

## Adding page links to mobile menu

Since the left sidebar will not be visible on mobile devices you need to manually add all your site pages to the links section inside a Super Navbar *(Site settings > Navbar > Navbar items)* so that they appear in a mobile menu. To make sure links don't appear in the navigation bar at the top, make sure to choose the **'Minimal'** Navbar type.

![Manually adding site links via a Super Navbar to appear on Mobile Menu](../../Resources%20Overview/Exclusive%20Deals%20and%20Discounts/Using%20Aether/mobile-links.gif)

Manually adding site links via a Super Navbar to appear on Mobile Menu

## Right sidebar

The right hand sidebar, by default features a table of contents so users can navigate your pages with ease. The table of contents is wrapped in a synced block so when you modify the content inside it will update throughout your site. You can also delete the column, it would not break the template layout.

## Primary color

You can easily switch the primary color of the Aether template to suite your needs. To change it, head to your site settings (cog icon), into the **'Code'** page and inside the **'CSS'** tab, simply paste the snippet below and update the HEX color code to your liking. The primary color acts like an accent color and appears in places like code syntax.

```css
:root {
	—-color-primary: #1ca0f1 !important;
}
```

## Call to action button

Aether has an additional feature that lets you turn a link into a call to action button, check out the example below.

[**Call to action button**](https://super.so)

For a link to become a button it must be a single **bold** text block on its own line with a link. You can customise the appearance of the button using the code snippet below. To change it, head to your site settings (cog icon), into the **'Code'** page and inside the **'CSS'** tab, simply paste the snippet below and update the values to your liking. This will also affect the styles of the Navbar call to action button.

```css
:root {
	--cta-color-text: #FFFFFF!important;
	--cta-color-bg: #1CA0F2!important;
	--cta-padding: 12px 22px!important;
  --cta-border-radius: 50px!important;
}
```

![Adding a call to action button](../../Resources%20Overview/Exclusive%20Deals%20and%20Discounts/Using%20Aether/call-to-action.gif)

Adding a call to action button

## Adding columns inside columns

You can now add multiple column blocks to Notion by using the Advanced blocks for multiple columns; type  `/2 columns` in Notion to add two columns.

## Custom site footer

Aether is compatible with the Super Footer. Add a footer to your site from the Footer menu on the Site dashboard in Super.

## Site footer social links

The site footer features a section to display social links, powered in Notion by a gallery block. By default the gallery block is loaded with a range of different Icons that are hidden. To view and enable them, hover over the gallery block and click filter button and uncheck the checkbox, this will show hidden icons. Then inside an icon update the link and check the 'show' box. Make sure to update the filter on the gallery block when you're finished.

![Updating footer social links](../../Resources%20Overview/Exclusive%20Deals%20and%20Discounts/Using%20Aether/footer-social.gif)

Updating footer social links

## Adding new pages

Adding new pages in Aether is easy. Inside the **Control panel toggle block** on the home page of Aether in Notion is a **Create page button**. Click this button to generate a new page using the Aether template and then move it into the left sidebar synced block. This template page automatically has the correct layout and contains all the synced blocks required for the template to work correctly.

![Adding new pages](../../Resources%20Overview/Exclusive%20Deals%20and%20Discounts/Using%20Aether/add-page.gif)

Adding new pages

## Content navigation footer

The content navigation footer is an optional section that you can see at the bottom of this section and it features two large buttons to help users navigate to the previous or next page. The links must be added manually and will only work correctly if it is placed at the very bottom of the center content column. To add this navigation footer to a page, head inside the **Control panel toggle block** on the home page of Aether in Notion and into the **tools page**. Inside is a **'Navigation footer'** template button that you can copy and paste into your other pages and simply click it ****to create a navigation footer.

![Adding a content navigation footer](../../Resources%20Overview/Exclusive%20Deals%20and%20Discounts/Using%20Aether/180e6efd-14f3-4f4e-aa18-56777d28825d_(1).gif)

Adding a content navigation footer
